title: lagochilus kschtutensis subsp. kschtutensis
intro: Lagochilus kschtutensis subsp. kschtutensis is a plant species of the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as Lagochilus, a type of mint that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lagochilus kschtutensis subsp. kschtutensis include its potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and exert antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Lagochilus kschtutensis subsp. kschtutensis typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
